channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump doubles planned tariffs on Canadian steel and aluminum to 50% as trade war intensifies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Tuesday that he will double his planned tariffs on steel and aluminum from 25% to 50% for Canada, escalating a trade war with the United States' northern neighbor.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, ROB GILLIES and MICHELLE PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Authorities give update on 'wrapped' body found in fishing nets
item.description: No foul play is suspected in the case of the "wrapped" deceased body that was pulled from the ocean by a fishing crew off the coast of Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: A plane carrying arrested ex-President Rodrigo Duterte leaves Manila en route to The Hague
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine officials say arrested former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte has left Manila on a plane and he will be turned over to the International Criminal Court in The Hague. A plane carrying Duterte left Manila late Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Police in the Dominican Republic say FBI joins search for US student who vanished on spring break
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — The FBI has joined the search for a 20-year-old University of Pittsburgh student who went missing nearly a week ago while on spring break in the Dominican Republic tourist town of Punta Cana, police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to increase steel and aluminum tariffs on Canada to 50 percent in response to electricity surcharge
item.description: The uncertainty around Trump's tariff plans have led to a downturn in the stock market, and the president has not ruled out the possibility of a recession this year.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Former US Rep. Katie Porter steps into crowded California governor's race
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Former Democratic U.S. House member Katie Porter announced Tuesday that she is entering the 2026 contest for California governor, joining a crowded field of candidates that could be upended if former Vice President Kamala Harris joins the race.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump overstepped his constitutional authority in freezing Congress' funding for USAID, judge says
item.description: A federal judge says President Donald Trump overstepped his authority in freezing almost all spending on U.S. humanitarian and development work abroad.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What is known about the deadly January air crash between a passenger jet and US Army helicopter
item.description: On Jan. 29, an American Airlines passenger jet and an Army helicopter collided as the plane prepared to land at Washington, D.C.’s Ronald Reagan National Airport. Both plunged into the Potomac River, killing all 67 people aboard.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he’s buying ‘brand new’ Tesla to support Musk
item.description: President Donald Trump said he would be buying a “brand new” Tesla to support his senior adviser Elon Musk, as the stock market shows shares of the electric car company tumbling.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 15 injured, 1 seriously, when school bus overturns in New Jersey: officials
item.description: At least 14 juveniles were injured when a school bus overturned on the Garden State Parkway in New Jersey on Monday, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: Trump considers easing safety screenings for chemicals
item.description: The Trump administration will consider easing regulations under which it considers the safety of existing chemicals, drawing concerns from public health advocates.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the Menendez brothers' bid for freedom
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The district attorney of Los Angeles says he does not support the resentencing of Lyle and Erik Menendez, brothers who have spent more than 30 years in prison for killing their parents at their Beverly Hills home in 1989.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Police arrest 4 in connection to hyperbaric chamber fire that killed 5-year-old Michigan boy: report
item.description: Police in Michigan have arrested four people after a 5-year-old boy receiving treatment at The Oxford Center died when the hyperbaric chamber he was in exploded.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans worry Trump's tariffs could harm economy
item.description: Republican lawmakers are growing alarmed over signs that President Trump’s expanding trade war is hurting the economy, something they’re hearing from constituents at home who are struggling to adapt to Trump’s zigzagging tariff pronouncements.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9

item.title: Education Department says 60 universities under investigation for antisemitism
item.description: The Department of Education’s Office of Civil Rights (OCR) is investigating 60 universities for antisemitism, according to letters sent on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: Computer owners duped in antivirus scam to get over $25M: What to know about payments
item.description: More than $25 million in payments will go out this week to customers who were tricked into paying for fraudulent tech support offered by two companies.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Social Security to again recoup 100% of overpayments
item.description: The Office of the Chief Actuary estimated the withholding rate increase could save about $7 billion over the next decade.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: Arrest of Palestinian activist stirs questions about protections for students and green card holders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The arrest of a Palestinian activist who helped organize campus protests of the war in Gaza has sparked questions about whether foreign students and green card holders are protected against being deported from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New CBP Home app assists migrants with self-deportation
item.description: Migrants hoping to avoid harsh consequences can convey their plans to voluntarily leave using a revised mobile app.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Massive ICE operation nabs nearly 650 illegal aliens in Texas, over 80% with criminal charges or convictions
item.description: ICE executed an operation in Houston that resulted in the arrest of 646 illegal aliens who were convicted of or charged with serious criminal offenses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: LA district attorney says he won’t support resentencing the Menendez brothers because they lied
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The district attorney of Los Angeles County said Monday that he does not support the resentencing of Lyle and Erik Menendez because the brothers have repeatedly lied about why they killed their parents at their Beverly Hills home in 1989.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk eyes Social Security and benefit programs for cuts while claiming widespread fraud
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk pushed debunked theories about Social Security on Monday while describing federal benefit programs as rife with fraud, suggesting they will be a primary target in his crusade to reduce government spending.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge halts effort to deport Columbia protest leader, sets Wednesday hearing
item.description: A federal judge on Monday scheduled a Wednesday court hearing for Mahmoud Khalil, a recent Columbia University graduate who was arrested by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), forbidding the government from moving forward with deportation efforts before then.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US added to human rights watchlist over Trump moves
item.description: The United States has been added to CIVICUS's monitor list of potential human rights threats due to President Trump's restrictive executive orders and institutional cutbacks, which are creating an atmosphere to chill democratic dissent.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WGN 9

item.title: Measles case confirmed in Maryland resident who recently travelled abroad
item.description: Early symptoms include a fever higher than 101 degrees, a runny nose, cough and red, watery eyes. A person will experience a red rash one to four days after early symptoms.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: Trump isn't alone in his geopolitical aspirations. Some in Illinois and Oregon want change too
item.description: As President Donald Trump floats grand ideas like reacquiring the Panama Canal and taking over Greenland, some rural residents of Illinois and Oregon are promoting geopolitical change of their own: They want to break away from their states, and perhaps unite with Indiana and Idaho.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fire risk remains high on Long Island after brush fires
item.description: The fire danger remains high on Long Island as officials work to quell wind-driven brush fires that broke out Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, WFLA, NBC

item.title: An early version of the Egg McMuffin included a surprising condiment
item.description: For a brief time, McDonald's envisioned their now-signature breakfast sandwich with a sweet component.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: See it: Activists vandalize Trump's Scotland golf resort
item.description: Pro-Palestinian activists said Saturday they vandalized one of Trump's golf resorts in Scotland in response to his proposal to empty the Gaza Strip of its Palestinian population.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Courts grapple with 'King' Trump
item.description: The White House sees few, if any, limits on President Trump's executive powers in his second term, but the federal court system is much less sure.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas bill could charge transgender people with 'gender identity fraud', if passed
item.description: A bill filed in the Texas House of Representatives this week threatens to charge transgender Texans with a crime for identifying with a gender that differs from their sex assigned at birth.
item.dc:creator: Cora Neas, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Daytona 500 winner William Byron claims NASCAR Cup pole
item.description: On Saturday, Daytona 500 winner William Byron claimed the Busch Light pole during the 312-mile race at Phoenix Raceway. According to NASCAR, it's the 14th pole of the 27 year-old's career.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, Fox 5

item.title: Couple charged after hundreds of living and dead rats found inside home
item.description: A Stark County couple is facing animal cruelty charges after authorities discovered horrific conditions inside their home, which included hundreds of living and dead rats.
item.dc:creator: Jack Shea, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ukraine supporters focus on hope and resilience as US relations sour and war carries on
item.description: The theme of “hope” was chosen long before this year’s deterioration of Washington-Kyiv relations, but participants at an international Ukrainian studies conference said that hope is needed more than ever — not only in Ukraine but in the United States itself.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, ABC 4
